days.title: Iceland — Reykjanes, Geysir & South Coast
days.meta: Jun 20–21 · Selfoss base · 🚗 358 km / 222 mi · ~2 days total

mapLinks.label: Day 1–2 Map — Iceland — Reykjanes, Geysir & South Coast

sections.heading: Day 1 — The South Coast Gateway
items: ⏰ 10:30am — Pick up Iceland car at KEF — Budget counter in Arrivals · shuttle bus from terminal to car lot (~10 min) · conf. #42334482US1 · allow 30–45 min for pickup formalities · fuel up at the KEF petrol station before leaving

⭐ 12:15pm — Kerð Crater — 45 min drive from KEF · red volcanic walls + vivid aquamarine lake · walk the rim (easy 15 min) + descend to the lake edge · fee: 600 ISK / ~€4.50 per person · allow 45 min · rarely crowded at this hour
🍽 1:15pm — Lunch in Selfoss — The Old Dairy (Mjólkurbúð) food hall for variety · or Kaffi Krús for cosy sit-down · allow ~1hr incl. supermarket stop

🛒 Grocery stop — Krónan or Bónus supermarket, Selfoss — do this right after lunch · pick up snacks + water + breakfast items for Day 2 · much cheaper than gas stations further east · last big supermarket before remote South Coast

⭐ ⭐ 2:45pm — Geysir & Strokkur geyser — 35 min from Selfoss · Strokkur erupts every 5–10 min · watch 2–3 eruptions then leave · stand on the NORTH side for the best photos (sun behind you) · Geysir itself rarely erupts but the steaming vents are impressive · free · allow 45 min

⭐ 4:15pm — Urriðafoss — hidden gem · 45 min south from Geysir · Iceland's most voluminous waterfall (more water than any other) · 2 min off Route 1 · almost no tourists · peaceful walk to the viewpoint · zero hiking required · free · allow 15 min · low effort / high reward

⭐ ⭐ 5:30pm — Seljalandsfoss — the famous "walk-behind" waterfall · bring waterproofs — you WILL get soaked walking behind it · parking: Parka app · ~1000 ISK / €7 · allow 1hr total · June 20 sun doesn't set until 12:03am — evening light is extraordinary here

⭐ ⭐ Gljúfrabúi — the hidden canyon waterfall — 500m north of Seljalandsfoss · most tour buses completely miss this · walk into the slot canyon and stand inside the waterfall chamber · narrow entrance — waterproofs essential · one of Iceland's most magical spots · free

⭐ 7:30pm — Skógafoss — drive 20 min east · climb the 527 stairs to the top for views of the coastline + rainbow spray · evening light from the west hits the falls perfectly at this hour · parking: Parka app · ~1000 ISK · allow 45 min

⭐ ⭐ 8:45pm — Reynisfjara black sand beach — 15 min east · basalt columns + Reynisdrangar sea stacks · ⚠️ Sneaker waves are deadly — never turn your back to the ocean · stay behind the safety rope · at 8:45pm in June = soft golden light · no tour buses · stunning for family photos · parking: Parka app · ~1000 ISK
📍 Vík village quick stop — 10 min east · red-roofed church on the hill viewpoint · 15 min only · fill the tank here if below half — N1 station in Vík
🏠 Room in Skeidflot — ✅ Night 1 · 📍 Skeidflot 871, Mýrdalur, Iceland · 📞 Airbnb · check in ~9:30pm · hosted by Arnar · $290.36

sections.heading: Day 2 — The Glacial Visuals Route
items: ⏰ 8:30am — Depart Skeidflot — slightly later start · no timed tours to rush for · fill water bottles · already 10km from Dyrhólaey — head west first before the big eastward push

⭐ 8:45am — Dyrhólaey arch — only 10km from Skeidflot · clifftop viewpoint over black sand coast + the sea arch · puffins nesting on Lágey islet in June — head to the lower Lágey area · 30 min max · worth it since you drive right past

⭐ ⭐ 10:30am — Fjaðrárgljúfur Canyon — 45 min drive east from Dyrhólaey · 100m deep serpentine moss-covered canyon · Game of Thrones Season 8 filming location · walk the full 2km rim path — best views at the far end · entry fee: ISK 1000 via Parka app · allow 1hr · genuinely otherworldly

🥾 12:00pm — Svartifoss, Skaftafell — "The Black Fall" · 30 min drive from canyon · unique basalt column waterfall — inspiration for Reykjavik's Hallgrímskirkja Cathedral · 1.5hr RT hike on well-marked path (5km · moderate) · far more unique than the glacier walk — you've done glaciers in Alaska · parking: Parka app · €7 · allow 2hrs total incl. walk

🍔 1:30pm — Lunch at Skaftafell Visitor Centre — café at the visitor centre · or food trucks in the car park (lobster soup is outstanding) · allow 45 min · use the toilets here — limited facilities further east

➕ Optional: Fjallsárlón — the quiet lagoon — 30 min east of Skaftafell · Jökulsárlón's "little brother" · much smaller + quieter · glacier face is dramatically close to the shore · free · 15 min stop · great if Jökulsárlón feels too crowded

⭐ ⭐ 3:00pm — Jökulsárlón Amphibian Boat Tour — the amphibian boat drives off dry land directly into the lagoon — kids love this moment · sail among icebergs glowing electric blue · taste 1,000-year-old glacier ice handed to you by the guide · watch for seals resting on the ice · English-speaking guide explains glacial geology · 35–40 min · all ages, no minimum height · ~$46–55/adult · ~$184 family total (saves ~$200 vs Zodiac) · book at icelagoon.is · no flotation suit needed

⭐ ⭐ 4:30pm — Diamond Beach — cross the bridge from Jökulsárlón · crystal ice blocks scattered across jet-black volcanic sand · every piece unique · allow 30–45 min · best photos: ice + black sand + crashing waves · ⚠️ Waves reach the beach — same sneaker wave warning as Reynisfjara
🚗 5:30pm — Start the drive back west — ~4.5hrs from Diamond Beach to BergOne · drive is mostly Ring Road (Route 1) — flat and straightforward · sun will still be up the entire drive
🍔 ~7:00pm — Dinner in Vík — Smiðjan Brugghsús (best burgers + fries in South Iceland) · or Suður-Vík · breaks the long drive perfectly · don't skip — nothing decent to eat near BergOne or KEF at 10pm
🏠 BergOne, Ytri-Njarðvík — ✅ Night 2 · 📍 1 Bergás, 260 Ytri-Njarðvík, Iceland · 📞 Booking.com · check in ~10:30pm · sun still up · $263.98 · ⏰ alarm 5:15am Jun 22 — FI568 departs KEF 7:20am

tags.text: 🚨 Book Jökulsárlón Amphibian Boat NOW — icelagoon.is · ~$46–55/adult · ~$184 for 4 · all ages · no minimum height · 35–40 min · 40 trips/day in high season but fills fast for Jun 21
📱 Parka app essential — download before landing · pay parking at Seljalandsfoss, Skógafoss, Reynisfjara via app · ~1000 ISK / €7 per stop · cameras read your plate — no cash option
☀️ 24-hour daylight advantage — Jun 20 sunset is 12:03am · Reynisfjara at 8:45pm = soft golden light + no tour buses · Seljalandsfoss at 5:30pm = rainbow in the spray · no rush ever
⚠️ Sneaker waves at Reynisfjara — Iceland's most dangerous beach · never turn your back to the ocean · stay well behind the safety rope · waves can appear from nowhere and travel far up the beach
⏰ Set alarm 5:15am Jun 22 — drive ~45 min to KEF · FI568 departs 7:20am · allow 1hr 45min at airport

☀️ Day 2 drive back — 4.5hrs from Diamond Beach to BergOne · stop for dinner in Vík (~7pm) · Smiðjan Brugghsús for burgers · sun is up the entire drive — no rushing needed

mapLinks.label: Day 3 Map — Fly → Lake Thun → Diemtigtal

items: ⏰ 5:45am — KEF Airport — return Budget rental car at the airport lot · shuttle back to terminal · allow 45 min for drop-off + check-in · FI568 check-in opens 5am · carry-on only — no checked bag wait
✈️ 7:20am — FI568 KEF → ZRH — Icelandair · 3hr 45min · arrives 1:05pm · clear Non-Schengen → Schengen passport control at ZRH · Hertz counter is in Arrivals Hall · allow 60–90 min for car pickup

🚗 1:05pm — Hertz ZRH pickup — conf. L5350959316 · Jeep Compass or similar · ⚠️ Ask at the counter: (1) Swiss motorway vignette included? (CHF 40 if not) · (2) Cross-border approval for Italy + Austria · (3) Photo / note the licence plate number — you need it tonight to buy the Austrian Digital Vignette · depart ZRH ~2:30–3:00pm

💳 Swiss motorway vignette — the 2026 sticker must be on the windscreen before driving on any Swiss motorway · Hertz should include it — if not, buy at any petrol station for CHF 40 · without it: CHF 200 on-the-spot fine

⭐ 4:15pm — Spiez Castle & Lake Thun — "the most beautiful bay in Europe" · Route 6 lakeside road from Interlaken — hugs Lake Thun shore · far more scenic than the motorway tunnel · Schloss Spiez gardens are free — skip the interior if tired · panoramic view: Lake Thun + Niesen pyramid mountain + Eiger on clear days · allow 30 min · perfect first Swiss lakes moment

🛒 Grocery stop — Coop Supermarket, Lauterbrunnen village — stop before check-in · closes ~6:30–7:00pm · better than Camping Jungfrau's own shop · pick up: Swiss cheese · charcuterie · local chocolate · wine · breakfast items · this is your first Alpine dinner — make it good · Aldi Interlaken visit can wait until Day 4 or 5 if needed

🏕 5:45pm — Check-in: Camping Jungfrau, Lauterbrunnen — ✅ Nights 3–4 · 📍 Weid 406, 3822 Lauterbrunnen, Switzerland · 📞 +41 33 856 20 10 · reception@camping-jungfrau.ch · check-in 15:00–18:30 · CHF 398 / $499 · 🚗 Park the car here — you won't need it again until Day 5 · valley is best by foot + train

⭐ ⭐ Staubbach Falls — evening walk — 5 min walk from Camping Jungfrau · 300m freefall straight over the village — one of Europe's highest free-falling waterfalls · in June: lit up at dusk · walk to the base and feel the mist · completely free · no driving · magical first Alpine evening · Veena will love the golden-hour light on the cliff

💳 Tonight: buy Austrian Digital Vignette — buy at asfinag.at · ~€16.50 · you need your licence plate number (noted at Hertz) · cameras on the Austrian motorway read the plate automatically · required before entering Austria on Jun 26
🍔 Dinner — picnic at campsite with Coop groceries · or Restaurant Jungfrau in the village (10 min walk) · early night — Oeschinensee gondola departs 8:30am tomorrow

days.title: Oeschinensee Heuberg Ridge + Grindelwald First — Double Gondola Day
days.meta: Jun 23 · Lauterbrunnen → Kandersteg (Oeschinensee) → Grindelwald First → Lauterbrunnen · 🚗 100 km / 62 mi · ~2h 30m incl. mountain roads

mapLinks.label: Day 4 Map — Oeschinensee + Grindelwald First — Double Gondola

items: ⏰ 7:30am — Depart Lauterbrunnen — 45 min scenic drive to Kandersteg via the Kandertal valley · bring packed picnic from Coop — fresh bread + Swiss cheese + fruit · no restaurant stop until Grindelwald
🚗 8:15am — Park at Oeschinen Talstation — Oeschinenstrasse 50, Kandersteg · parking ~CHF 8 · arrive early to guarantee a space · car park fills by 10am in summer

⭐ ⭐ 8:30am — Oeschinensee gondola — ✅ Pre-booked · Order 254-SK2MH6 · CHF 125 · arrive at talstation by 8:15am · first lift = best chance of mirror-like reflections on the lake before the wind picks up

🥾 Heuberg Ridge Hike — Trail #8 (Priority) — from the gondola top follow signs for Läger then Heuberg · moderate · ~3hrs full loop · NOT the direct lakeside path · ⚠️ Some steep drop-offs near the cliffs — if anyone has severe vertigo stick to lower Trail #1 · the upper route gives the famous "bird's eye" view of the turquoise lake — far more dramatic

⭐ ⭐ 10:00am — Heuberg Panoramic Viewpoint — arrive at the Heuberg cliffs · the lake appears 400m below you in full turquoise glory · Blüemlisalp glacier massif fills the horizon · one of the most dramatic alpine viewpoints in Switzerland · perfect picnic spot · allow 30 min
🥾 11:00am — Descent to lakeside — hike down to the shore · touch the glacial water (ice cold even in June) · rowing boats available to hire · picnic lunch here · allow 1hr total

🚗 12:00pm — Depart Kandersteg → Grindelwald — cable car down first · 1hr 15min drive · park at Parkhaus Eiger+ or Sportzentrum near Firstbahn · ~CHF 10–15 · if both full: Grindelwald Grund → short train/bus up to Firstbahn · arrive ~1:15pm
⭐ ⭐ 1:45pm — Grindelwald Firstbahn gondola — ✅ Booking #238070 · 2 adults + 2 children · ride to First (2,168m) · Eiger north face directly opposite · spectacular Bernese Alps panorama

⭐ ⭐ 2:15pm — First Cliff Walk — suspended steel + glass walkway bolted to the sheer cliff face · Tissot-sponsored glass floor section — walk over 45m of air · vertigo-inducing but perfectly safe · allow 30 min · views of Grindelwald valley + Eiger + Wetterhorn

🥾 2:45pm — Bachalpsee hike — Trail from First station · 3km / 1.9mi one-way · gentle gravel path · ~45 min each way · ☁️ Even in late June: small snow patches possible — wear hiking boots with grip · cows with cowbells in the meadows by this date — classic Swiss mountain atmosphere

⭐ 3:45pm — Bachalpsee "Blue Jewel" — Schreckhorn + Finsteraarhorn reflected in the still water · sit on the bank · allow 30–45 min · on calm days the Eiger reflection is perfect · turn back by 4:30pm latest to make the last gondola
⚠️ 5:00pm — Back at First station — LAST gondola down is 6:00pm — do not miss it · allow 45 min walk back from Bachalpsee · leave the lake by 4:30pm at the absolute latest
🚗 5:30pm — Gondola down → drive back — 20 min drive to Camping Jungfrau, Lauterbrunnen · arrive ~6:30pm
🍔 Dinner — campsite kitchen with Coop groceries · or Airtime Restaurant in Lauterbrunnen village (10 min walk) · early night — Day 5 is the long drive to Berchtesgaden

days.title: Lauterbrunnen → Berchtesgaden — The Cross-Country Push
days.meta: Jun 24 · Lauterbrunnen → Staubbach Falls → Trümmelbach Falls → Berchtesgaden · 🚗 595 km / 370 mi · ~6h non-stop

items: ⏰ 8:30am — Checkout Camping Jungfrau — pack the car the night before · long drive day — be on the road by 8:30am · walk to Trümmelbach Falls first (3 min drive or 15 min walk from the campsite)

⭐ ⭐ 8:30am — Trümmelbach Falls — be first at the gate when it opens at 9:00am · 10 subterranean glacial waterfalls thundering inside the mountain · lift takes you up inside the rock · spray is intense — wear waterproofs · ~€12/adult · allow 45 min · unlike anything else in the Alps · only 3 min drive from Camping Jungfrau — no excuse to skip this

📸 Lauterbrunnen valley exit photo stop — pull over on the B532 heading north out of the village · look back south into the valley — 72 waterfalls cascading down both walls with Jungfrau behind · 5 min · zero detour · your last look at the Bernese Alps
☕ 10:00am — Final Lauterbrunnen coffee — quick espresso in the village before the motorway · Airtime Café or Camping Jungfrau café · allow 20 min
🚗 10:45am — Depart Lauterbrunnen → Berchtesgaden — ~595 km · ~6hrs driving · longest drive day of the trip · route: Interlaken → Bern → Zurich → Bregenz → Munich area → Berchtesgaden

⚠️ Austrian Pfändertunnel near Bregenz — you clip a corner of Austria near Bregenz on the standard route · your Austrian Digital Vignette (bought Day 3 at asfinag.at) is required here · without it: heavy on-the-spot fine · cameras check automatically · no toll booth to stop at

⛽ Fuel strategy — fill up in Austria or Germany — Swiss fuel is significantly more expensive · first opportunity: Bregenz area (Austria) · last cheap option before Berchtesgaden: Rosenheim or Bad Reichenhall (Germany)

📍 12:30pm — Lunch stop: Vaduz, Liechtenstein — 45 min "Country #3" stop · walk the main square (Städtle) · Vaduz Castle visible on the hill above — official residence of the Prince · lunch at a café in the centre · Liechtenstein is one of only two doubly-landlocked countries in the world — kids will love the stamp · get your passport stamped at the tourist office for 3 CHF · allow 45 min total

⚠️ A8 Construction Warning 2026 — A8 between Ulm and Munich is under major renovation in 2026 · check Google Maps live before Munich · if showing 60+ min red delay: switch to the scenic B31/B12 southern route (Deutsche Alpenstraße) through the German Alps · slower but beautiful — avoids stop-and-go misery · Wednesday = moderate traffic risk (not as bad as Fri/Sat)

🛒 Grocery stop — REWE or Lidl, Lindau or Berchtesgaden — cross into Germany at Lindau — first stop after Switzerland · German groceries 30–40% cheaper than Swiss · stock up for Days 5–6 (two nights in Berchtesgaden) · or use REWE Berchtesgaden on arrival (Maximilianstr. 26) · open until 8pm

📸 Chiemsee photo stop — Bernau Exit 106 — exit A8 at Bernau · 2km to Felden lakeshore · Bavaria's largest lake — "Bavarian Sea" · Alps reflected in flat water · Fraueninsel visible · allow 15 min · worth the detour on this long drive

📸 ~5:00pm — Hintersee lake photo stop — 15 min drive from Berchtesgaden town · turquoise lake + Watzmann massif + giant moss-covered Zauberwald boulders · one of the most photogenic spots in Bavaria · free · allow 20 min · park at Seeklause car park
🏠 ~5:30pm — Check-in: One-Bedroom Apartment, Berchtesgaden — ✅ Nights 5–6 · 📍 Kälbersteinstraße 4, 83471 Berchtesgaden, Germany · 📞 Booking.com message host · check-in 16:00–20:00
🍔 Dinner — Berchtesgaden — Bräustüberl Berchtesgaden (historic brewery restaurant, Bräuhausstraße 13) · or Gasthof Neuhaus · allow 1hr · early night — Königssee at 9:15am tomorrow

days.title: Königssee · Eagle's Nest · Hintersee — Lakes, Legends & Luxury
days.meta: Jun 25 · Berchtesgaden → Königssee boat → Eagle’s Nest → Hintersee → Berchtesgaden · 🚗 80 km / 50 mi · ~2h total

mapLinks.label: Day 6 Map — Munich → Königssee → Eagle's Nest → Hintersee → Hallein

items: ⏰ 8:15am — Königssee Seelände car park — only 10 min drive from Berchtesgaden apartment · car park is huge but the dock is a 10 min walk from the lot — factor this in · aim for the 8:30 or 9:00am boat · buy tickets at the dock counter

⭐ ⭐ 8:30am — Königssee electric boat — completely silent electric boats — only powered vessels allowed on this lake · ⚠️ Go all the way to Salet (the final stop) — NOT St. Bartholomä · 55 min to Salet · ~€28–30 return to Salet per adult · book at seenschifffahrt.de ~1 week before · Jun 25 is peak season

🎺 The mountain echo tradition — midway the captain stops and plays a trumpet · the sound bounces off the sheer cliff walls · a Königssee tradition for centuries · have €1–2 ready to tip · kids will love this moment

🥾 9:45am — Obersee & Röthbachfall walk — from Salet dock: flat 15 min walk to Obersee · mirror-like glacial lake with the iconic half-submerged wooden boathouse · continue to the end of the lake for Röthbachfall — one of the tallest waterfalls in Germany crashing into the lake · completely peaceful — day-trippers rarely make it this far · allow 1.5hrs total

🍺 12:00pm — St. Bartholomä lunch stop — board the boat back from Salet · stop at St. Bartholomä · beer garden at the historic red-domed hunting lodge · Bavarian classics: Brotzeit plate + Weißbier · unique setting right on the lakeshore · allow 45 min · then final boat back

📍 Dokumentation Obersalzberg WWII Museum — ➕ Optional · 30 min · €10/adult · the history of the Nazi elite's mountain retreat is extraordinary context for the Eagle's Nest visit · do this while waiting for the bus if there's a queue

⭐ ~4:00pm — Rossfeld Panorama Road — right next to Eagle's Nest area · €9 toll · Germany's highest panoramic toll road at 1,570m · at the summit car park: straddle the stone border marker between Germany and Austria · 360° views: Hoher Göll · Kehlstein · Watzmann · Berchtesgaden valley · allow 45 min · no booking needed

🥾 ~4:45pm — Hintersee & Zauberwald (Magic Forest) — 15 min drive toward Ramsau · crystal-clear turquoise lake · follow the trail into the Zauberwald · flat easy walk through ancient forest along a mountain stream · perfectly shaded — ideal afternoon cooldown after a big day · Watzmann reflected in the lake · allow 45 min · free

days.title: The Roof of Austria — Grossglockner High Alpine Road → Sillian
days.meta: Jun 28 · Bad Goisern → Grossglockner High Alpine Road → Lienz → Sillian, Austria · 🚗 280 km / 174 mi · ~4h 30m incl. Grossglockner slow drive

⭐ ⭐ Grossglockner High Alpine Road — 48km of switchbacks across the highest surfaced alpine pass in Austria · Hohe Tauern National Park · road open from 5:30am — last entry 45 min before 9pm closing · check road status at grossglockner.at before departing — occasional snow closures in June

💳 9:30am — Ferleiten Toll Gate — 2026 rate: €46.50/car · buy a "Day Ticket" online in advance at grossglockner.at to skip the payment queue · card accepted · start the 36 hairpin turns · first stop: Edelweiß-Spitze — go there FIRST before the motorcycles arrive

📍 Haus Alpine Naturschau — natural history museum — ➕ Optional · 15 min · halfway up the road · modern museum about the High Alpine ecology of Hohe Tauern National Park · great context for the kids before reaching the summit

⭐ ⭐ 10:15am — Edelweiß-Spitze (2,571m) — MUST STOP · dead-end cobblestone branch road — go here FIRST · if you wait until afternoon the narrow road is congested with motorcycles · highest car-accessible point on the entire road · 37 mountain peaks visible on a clear day including the Grossglockner (3,798m) · straddle the viewing platform for 360° panorama · allow 20 min

📸 11:30am — Hochtor Tunnel (2,504m) — highest point of the road surface · stop for a photo at the border between the provinces of Salzburg and Carinthia · a small stone marks the exact boundary · 10 min stop

⭐ ⭐ 12:15pm — Kaiser-Franz-Josefs-Höhe (2,369m) — the grand finale · Pasterze Glacier viewing platform · Austria's longest glacier (8km) stretching toward the Grossglockner · ⚠️ 2026: glacier has receded significantly — to touch the ice take the Gletscherbahn funicular down + hike 30–45 min · if tired from Day 8 just enjoy the view from the terrace · ibex often visible on the rocks · allow 1hr

🐺 Marmot & ibex spotting — Gamsgrubenweg — from Kaiser-Franz-Josefs-Höhe: follow the Gamsgrubenweg tunnel path for 10 min · best place on the entire road to see wild marmots up close · occasionally ibex too · kids will love this · free · bring binoculars if you have them

🍴 1:30pm — Lunch at Kaiser-Franz-Josefs-Höhe — Restauration Kaiser-Franz-Josefs-Höhe · Tiroler Gröstl + Gulasch · panoramic terrace facing the glacier · watch for marmots on the rocks while eating · allow 1hr

⚠️ Descent toward Heiligenblut — brake warning — use engine braking (low gear) on the descent · do NOT ride the brakes continuously — they will overheat and smoke before the bottom · shift down to 2nd or 3rd gear and let the engine slow you

⭐ 3:30pm — Heiligenblut village — iconic pilgrimage church with Grossglockner in the background · 📸 Best photo: upper car park near the cemetery · Grossglockner peak frames perfectly behind the church spire · 4pm light is ideal · allow 30 min · inside the church: sacred blood relic from which the village takes its name

➕ Optional: Loacker Café, Bolzano — only worth doing if arriving Sillian before 5pm · South Tyrolean wafer + chocolate brand · flagship café on Piazza Walther · espresso + Loacker wafers + krapfen · 20 min detour off route
